verses.text: The beloved praises her eyes, hair, teeth, and lips, finding her wholly lovely. He delights in every feature. Christ looks upon His people and sees a beauty that His own grace has given them.
The beloved praises her eyes, hair, teeth, and lips, finding her wholly lovely. He delights in every feature. Christ looks upon His people and sees a beauty that His own grace has given them.
The beloved praises her eyes, hair, teeth, and lips, finding her wholly lovely. He delights in every feature. Christ looks upon His people and sees a beauty that His own grace has given them.
Her neck is like the tower of David hung with shields, her grace strong and stately. Beauty and dignity meet in her. The Church adorned by her Lord is both lovely and majestic.
Her neck is like the tower of David hung with shields, her grace strong and stately. Beauty and dignity meet in her. The Church adorned by her Lord is both lovely and majestic.

He resolves to dwell on the mountain of myrrh, calls her 'all fair' with no spot, and bids his spouse come with him from the heights. He sees her flawless. To Christ His people appear without blemish, clothed in His righteousness.

He resolves to dwell on the mountain of myrrh, calls her 'all fair' with no spot, and bids his spouse come with him from the heights. He sees her flawless. To Christ His people appear without blemish, clothed in His righteousness.

He resolves to dwell on the mountain of myrrh, calls her 'all fair' with no spot, and bids his spouse come with him from the heights. He sees her flawless. To Christ His people appear without blemish, clothed in His righteousness.

'Thou hast ravished my heart, my sister, my spouse,' he says; her love is better than wine, her lips drop honey. The beloved is captivated by her love. Christ delights in the love and worship of His people.

'Thou hast ravished my heart, my sister, my spouse,' he says; her love is better than wine, her lips drop honey. The beloved is captivated by her love. Christ delights in the love and worship of His people.

'Thou hast ravished my heart, my sister, my spouse,' he says; her love is better than wine, her lips drop honey. The beloved is captivated by her love. Christ delights in the love and worship of His people.

She is 'a garden enclosed... a fountain of gardens,' fragrant with every choice fruit and spice. She is set apart and fruitful for him alone. The believer's heart is a garden kept for Christ, bearing the fruits of grace.

She is 'a garden enclosed... a fountain of gardens,' fragrant with every choice fruit and spice. She is set apart and fruitful for him alone. The believer's heart is a garden kept for Christ, bearing the fruits of grace.

She is 'a garden enclosed... a fountain of gardens,' fragrant with every choice fruit and spice. She is set apart and fruitful for him alone. The believer's heart is a garden kept for Christ, bearing the fruits of grace.

She is 'a garden enclosed... a fountain of gardens,' fragrant with every choice fruit and spice. She is set apart and fruitful for him alone. The believer's heart is a garden kept for Christ, bearing the fruits of grace.
She calls the winds to blow upon her garden and bids her beloved come and eat its fruits. She offers all she has to him. The soul invites Christ to enjoy the graces He Himself has planted.
